title: Crispy Springy Thai Fry Rice
description: Peppery and light Thai dish featuring tomato ketchup, made with crispy Thai fried rice and a dash of fresh herbs.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large copper pot over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the Thai red curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the cooked rice to the pot and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, breaking up any clumps.
Add the fish sauce (if using) and tomato ketchup, and stir-fry for another minute.
Add the peanut oil and stir-fry for 1 minute, until the rice is crispy and golden brown.
Transfer the rice mixture to a serving platter and garnish with thinly sliced spring onions and chopped cilantro.
Note: You can also add cooked shrimp or chicken to make this dish more substantial.
Tips: Use a non-stick copper pot to prevent the rice from sticking and making a mess.
Tips: Adjust the amount of curry paste to your desired level of spiciness.
